A technical review of ten essential tools for your Mac workflow, ranging from gesture-based automation to local AI-powered file management.
May 2026 brought a significant wave of innovation for macOS power users. From utilities that tap into the internal accelerometer to local AI models that reshape file organization, these ten tools were selected based on utility, performance, and impact on daily development and creative workflows.
1. Pica: Native Font Management
Pica provides a streamlined, native interface for managing extensive font collections. Its ability to monitor watch folders for automatic updates and organize assets into color-coded collections makes it a performant alternative to heavier design suites.
2. Curflow & Tapcut: Gesture-Based Control
Curflow and Tapcut represent the new ceiling for macOS input automation. Curflow translates trackpad gestures into OS-level commands, while Tapcut utilizes the MacBook accelerometer to map custom functions (like triggering IDE scripts or launching Claude) to specific tap sequences on the palm rest.
3. PeekFocus
Press enter or click to view image in full size

PeekFocus is a utility that creates a focus state through architectural blur. With a single keystroke, it shifts view layers to dim background ambient noise, supporting multi-monitor setups and offering customizable frosted effects.
4. Filect: Localized AI Parsing
Filect leverages local AI models to audit and sort file systems. Its semantic search capabilities allow users to query folders using natural language requests, bypassing the need for manual meta-tagging.
5. PaceBar
PaceBar acts as a telemetry tool for human performance. It monitors app switching patterns and activity intensity, providing real-time data to help developers identify cognitive load thresholds and schedule necessary intervals for recovery.
6. Manuscripts.app: Academic Workflow
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Addressing the limitations of spreadsheets for research tracking, Manuscripts handles the iterative nature of academic publishing, from drafting to reviewer feedback management, entirely offline.
7. Edit Mind: Localized Video Search
Edit Mind 3 is an essential tool for high-volume content creators, enabling semantic search over hours of raw footage without cloud dependency, ensuring privacy and low-latency integration into existing NLE workflows.
8. Cats Lock & Keeby
Cats Lock provides a granular sandboxed kill-switch for accidental key presses, while Keeby utilizes spatial audio processing to simulate mechanical keyboard acoustics, featuring sample data from Gateron and Alps switches for a reactive, offline typing experience.

Best Mac products of the month May 2026
